fields.summary: Add API/Implementation to YarnClient to retrieve label-to-node mapping
(Clone of YARN-796) Allow for (admin) labels on nodes and resource-requests

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Provide API for client to retrieve label to node mapping

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

fields.summary: NodeLabelsManager implementation to retrieve label to node mapping

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

comments.body: Converted this and YARN-3075 to sub task of YARN-2492. And suggest to rename their titles: They're not simply client/server, should be YarnClient and NodeLabelsManager, YarnClient itself contains client and server (ClientRMService).

{color:red}-1 overall{color}.  Here are the results of testing the latest attachment 
  http://issues.apache.org/jira/secure/attachment/12696366/YARN-3076.001.patch
  against trunk revision 26dee14.

    {color:green}+1 @author{color}.  The patch does not contain any @author tags.

    {color:green}+1 tests included{color}.  The patch appears to include 4 new or modified test files.

    {color:green}+1 javac{color}.  The applied patch does not increase the total number of javac compiler warnings.

    {color:green}+1 javadoc{color}.  There were no new javadoc warning messages.

    {color:green}+1 eclipse:eclipse{color}.  The patch built with eclipse:eclipse.

    {color:green}+1 findbugs{color}.  The patch does not introduce any new Findbugs (version 2.0.3) warnings.

    {color:green}+1 release audit{color}.  The applied patch does not increase the total number of release audit warnings.

    {color:red}-1 core tests{color}.  The patch failed these unit tests in hadoop-mapreduce-project/hadoop-mapreduce-client/hadoop-mapreduce-client-jobclient hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-api hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-client hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-common hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-server/hadoop-yarn-server-resourcemanager:

                  org.apache.hadoop.conf.TestJobConf
                  org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.TestLargeSort

Test results: https://builds.apache.org/job/PreCommit-YARN-Build/6503//testReport/
Console output: https://builds.apache.org/job/PreCommit-YARN-Build/6503//console

Above 2 test failures are unrelated. Mapreduce issues raised for both
[~leftnoteasy], kindly review
Patch cannot apply on trunk, but use {{git apply -3}} can cleanly apply, upload patch on behalf of [~varun_saxena] to kick Jenkins

comments.body: Converted this and YARN-3075 to sub task of YARN-2492. And suggest to rename their titles: They're not simply client/server, should be YarnClient and NodeLabelsManager, YarnClient itself contains client and server (ClientRMService).
